Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Team South Africa: Here’s when to watch the 4x100m relay final on Friday, 9 August

Team South Africa are involved in four sports at the 2024 Paris Olympics on Friday, 9 August. Here’s who to keep an eye out for …

Team South Africaare involved infoursports at the2024 Paris OlympicsonFriday, 9 August.

Enjoy the Paris Olympic Games 2024 experience at your favourite ATKV Resort!

They started the day withfourmedals, but look to have realistic hopes of adding to that in the men’s 4x100m relay at the Stade de France on Friday night.

The United States (30 golds) lead China (29 golds), Australia (18), France (14) and Great Britain (13) in the medal standings.

As reported byThe South African website, Team SA are41stwith one gold (Tatjana Smith), one silver (Smith) and two bronzes (men’s Sevens rugby and Alan Hatherly).

For the latest Paris Olympics 2024 news, bookmarkThe South African website’sdedicated section for free-to-read content

Men’s 4x400m relay, heats (11:17):Team SA

How it works: There are 2 heats of 9 countries in each. The first 3 in each heat, plus the 2 fastest, go through to the final

Men’s 800m semi-finals (11:40):Edmund du Plessis

How it works: Du Plessis is in the second of three semi-finals. The first 2 and the next fastest 2 progress to the final

Women’s 100m hurdles, semi-finals (11:35):Marione Fourie

How it works: Fourie is in the third of three semi-finals. The first 2 and the next fastest 2 progress to the final

Men’s 4x100m relay, final (19:47):Team SA

Women’s 3m springboard final (15:00):Julia Vincent

Women’s individual, third round (09:44):Paula Reto is on 151 at halfway after rounds of 78 and 73.

Women’s individual, third round (12:06):Ashleigh Buhai is on 141 at halfway after rounds of 68 and 73.

Men’s double kayak 500m semi-finals (11:10):Hamish Lovemore and Andrew Birkett

How it works: There are eight boats in each of the 2 semi-finals. The first four in each race go through to the final
